alignments.pub_claim_text: GIN can generate virtual patients that are visually authentic and pathophysiologically interpretable.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about virtual patients is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CLAIRE is a distributed-memory implementation of an effective solver for constrained large deformation diffeomorphic image registration problems in three dimensions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CLAIRE is a distributed-memory implementation of an effective solver for constrained large deformation diifeomorphic image registration problems in three dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is the same aside from a minor typographical difference; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate registration accuracy on several neuroimaging datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solver demonstrates registration accuracy on several neuroimaging datasets.
alignments.rationale: The result about registration accuracy is carried verbatim into the publication with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve a speedup of (on average) 5× with a peak performance of up to 17× compared to our former work.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves a speedup of (on average) 5× with a peak performance of up to 17× compared to former work.
alignments.rationale: The performance speedup numbers and comparison target are the same in both versions, so this is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We report scalability results on state-of-the-art supercomputing platforms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solver can solve registration problems for clinically relevant data sizes in two to four minutes on a standard compute node with 20 cores, attaining excellent data fidelity.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a generic statement about scalability on supercomputers with a concrete runtime on a standard node for clinically relevant sizes; this clarifies and broadens applicability to more typical hardware and reports stronger (higher-certainty) performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kriging provides a solution for approximating an unknown function by interpolating training data based on prior kernel specification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed numerical approximation approach to kernel selection allows for the construction of kernels that maintain accuracy even when the number of interpolation points is halved.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general statement about kriging is reformulated in the publication to a specific numerical approximation/kernel-selection result focused on robustness to halved interpolation points, a more specific technical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach allows for the training of very deep networks by integrating a stochastic data-driven dynamical system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep networks trained using the proposed approach can achieve accurate classification from only one data point per class.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes empirical capability (one-shot classification) of deep networks trained with the approach rather than the method's dynamical-systems framing; the claim is clarified toward demonstrable performance and reported with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep networks can achieve accurate classification from only one data point per class.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep networks trained using the proposed approach can achieve accurate classification from only one data point per class.
alignments.rationale: This result appears verbatim in the publication and is reported with higher certainty, so the substantive claim is preserved and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Minimizing approximation errors by halving random subsets of interpolation points outperforms training with relative entropy and dropout for kernels parameterized by CNN weights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Minimizing approximation errors by halving random subsets of interpolation points outperforms training the same CNN architecture with relative entropy and dropout.
alignments.rationale: The experimental comparison and its outcome are the same in preprint and publication, with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The networks learn archetypes of each class and manipulate distances between points in different and the same classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep networks appear to learn archetypes of each class and can expand distances between points in different classes while contracting distances between points in the same class.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the description of learned geometry but retains the same core finding about archetypes and intra-/inter-class distances.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural models outperform feature-based models on two benchmark biomedical corpora, GENIA and CRAFT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural models outperform feature-based models on two benchmark biomedical corpora, GENIA and CRAFT.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Better intrinsic parsing performance does not always imply better extrinsic event extraction performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Better intrinsic parsing performance does not always imply better extrinsic event extraction performance.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about intrinsic vs extrinsic performance appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a detailed empirical comparison of traditional feature-based and neural network-based models for POS tagging and dependency parsing in the biomedical context.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a detailed empirical comparison of traditional feature-based and neural network-based models for POS tagging and dependency parsing in the biomedical context.
alignments.rationale: The stated contribution is unchanged between the preprint and the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods for anomaly detection in images can be classified mainly by the structural assumption they make on the 'normal' image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Five different structural assumptions emerge for the background model in anomaly detection methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the classification by enumerating five structural assumptions, adding specificity to the preprint's general classification claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis leads to the reformulation of the best representative algorithms by attaching an a contrario detection that controls the number of false positives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed uniform detection scheme controls the number of false positives in anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: Both describe a detection scheme that controls false positives; the publication reframes the mechanism more uniformly but preserves the original intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generic algorithms proposed in the study seem to generalize or reconcile most methods for anomaly detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several generic algorithms are proposed that generalize or reconcile most existing anomaly detection methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution: generic algorithms that generalize/reconcile existing methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The six best representatives of the proposed classes of algorithms were compared on anomalous images and a synthetic database.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding comparison-by-six-representatives claim appears in the publication's listed claims, indicating this specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to perform automatic anomaly detection on a single image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to perform automatic anomaly detection on a single image.
alignments.rationale: The claim is retained verbatim but the publication lists lower certainty (medium) than the preprint (high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-robot approaches are preferable for large scale coverage operations compared to single robot approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-robot approaches are preferable for large scale coverage operations compared to single robot approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion directly with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both multi-robot coverage and Dubins vehicle coverage are NP-complete problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both multi-robot coverage and Dubins vehicle coverage are NP-complete problems.
alignments.rationale: The statement about NP-completeness is carried over identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed heuristics methods based on k-TSP formulation and clustering algorithms efficiently solve the problem of complete coverage by a multi-robot team of Dubins vehicles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed heuristics methods based on a variant of the traveling salesman problem-k-TSP-formulation and clustering algorithms efficiently solve the coverage problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the formulation as a variant of the TSP/k-TSP but preserves the claim of effective heuristics for coverage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods have been tested in simulations and with a team of ASVs operating on a lake to ensure their applicability in real world.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods have been tested in simulations and with a team of ASVs on a 200 km2 lake to ensure their applicability in the real world.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a concrete 200 km2 lake specification and raises certainty to high, amplifying the claim's confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All four visualization methods focus on brain regions known to be involved in Alzheimer's disease, such as inferior and middle temporal gyrus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All four visualization methods focus on brain regions known to be involved in Alzheimer’s disease, such as inferior and middle temporal gyrus.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The occlusion-based methods focus more on specific regions, while the gradient-based methods pick up distributed relevance patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The occlusion-based methods focus more on specific regions, while the gradient-based methods pick up distributed relevance patterns.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distribution of relevance varies across patients, with some having a stronger focus on the temporal lobe, whereas for others more cortical areas are relevant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distribution of relevance varies across patients, with some having a stronger focus on the temporal lobe, whereas for others more cortical areas are relevant.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same patient-level variability result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying different visualization methods is important to understand the decisions of a CNN, which is crucial to increase clinical impact and trust in computer-based decision support systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying different visualization methods is important to understand the decisions of a CNN, which is crucial to increase clinical impact and trust in computer-based decision support systems.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the importance of multiple visualization methods is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Module dependencies can be predicted before they actually appear in a software system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Module dependencies can be predicted before they actually appear in a software system.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this predictive conclusion directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The link prediction approach is feasible for identifying new package dependencies in Java projects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The link prediction approach is feasible for identifying new package dependencies in Java projects.
alignments.rationale: The feasibility result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work opens opportunities for further development of software-specific strategies for dependency prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work opens opportunities for further development of software-specific strategies for dependency prediction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement about future opportunities is preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing tools are limited to detecting undesired dependencies only after they have appeared in the system.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit limitation of existing tools that was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method provides increased accuracy and robustness over state-of-the-art visual odometry algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method provides increased accuracy and robustness over state-of-the-art visual odometry algorithms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for using the full area of the input image even with strong distortion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model parameters within an active keyframe window are jointly optimized, including intrinsic/extrinsic camera parameters, 3D position of points, and affine brightness parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model parameters within an active keyframe window are jointly optimized, including intrinsic/extrinsic camera parameters and three-dimensional position of points.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method extends direct sparse odometry by using the unified omnidirectional model as a projection function.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a method-specific formulation detail (unified omnidirectional model) that was not stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Brussels operational-realistic approach to quantum physics and quantum cognition provides a fundamental strategy for modeling the meaning associated with collections of documental entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Brussels operational-realistic approach to quantum physics and quantum cognition provides a fundamental strategy for modeling the meaning associated with collections of documental entities.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim texts are identical in content and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distinction between the World Wide Web and the Quantum Web (QWeb) is crucial for understanding the relationship between printed documents and abstract meaning entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distinction between the World Wide Web and the Quantum Web (QWeb) is crucial for understanding the relationship between printed documents and abstract meaning entities.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim in the publication with the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The superposition principle and associated interference effects are insufficient to model all experimental probabilistic data related to document word counts and co-occurrences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The superposition principle and associated interference effects are insufficient to model all experimental probabilistic data related to document word counts and co-occurrences.
alignments.rationale: The result statement is carried over exactly from preprint to publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Context effects must be considered in addition to quantum effects to accurately model the probabilistic data obtained from document analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Context effects must be considered in addition to quantum effects to accurately model the probabilistic data obtained from documents.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose an efficient, gradient-free algorithm for solving empirical risk-minimization tasks using ensemble Kalman inversion (EKI).
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions with identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the wide applicability and robustness of the proposed algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the wide applicability and robustness of the proposed algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The reported numerical-results claim is carried over verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach includes applications in offline and online supervised learning with deep neural networks, as well as graph-based semi-supervised learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is applicable to problems with non-differentiable loss functions, where back-propagation is not possible.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim about applicability to non-differentiable losses is not present in the preprint and is therefore a newly added claim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms have achieved state-of-the-art performance in single image super-resolution (SISR).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms have achieved state-of-the-art performance in single image super-resolution (SISR).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Several critical limitations of baseline methods in SISR are summarized and discussed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review concludes with a discussion of current challenges and future trends in SISR using deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current challenges and future trends in SISR leveraging deep learning algorithms are outlined in the conclusion of the review.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion content is equivalent though wording is adjusted; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study explores the use of optimized black-box models based on artificial neural networks to model fish behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a methodology using artificial neural networks to model fish individual and collective behaviors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed models can be implemented as robot controllers to form mixed-groups of fish and robots.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed models can be implemented as robot controllers to form mixed-groups of fish and robots.
alignments.rationale: The result about robot controller implementation is preserved verbatim and thus unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology presented utilizes multilayer perceptron or echo state networks optimized through evolutionary algorithms to accurately model fish individual and collective behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology optimizes multilayer perceptron or echo state networks through evolutionary algorithms to accurately model fish behaviors.
alignments.rationale: The methodological details and claim of accurate modeling are consistent between versions, with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated models are assessed for biomimetism and compared to fish experimental behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The artificial neural networks used in the study are bio-inspired rather than biomimetic.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims biomimetism assessment, while the publication downgrades this to 'bio-inspired rather than biomimetic', which revises and narrows the original claim and reduces the strength of the biomimetic claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach uses pretrained classifiers to visually guide users to noisy labels, allowing for interactive checking of error candidates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach uses pretrained classifiers to visually guide users to noisy labels, allowing for interactive checking of error candidates.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried forward verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The categorization of labeling errors into three different types is based on an analysis of potential pitfalls in label acquisition processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The categorization of labeling errors into three different types is based on an analysis of potential pitfalls in label acquisition processes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has been successfully used to spot errors in well-known machine learning benchmark data sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach has been successfully used to spot errors in well-known machine learning benchmark data sets.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is unchanged in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The techniques presented are independent of the classification algorithm and can be extended to many other types of training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The techniques presented are independent of the classification algorithm and can be extended to many other types of training data.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about generality is preserved; wording and emphasis remain effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed attention-based hierarchical LSTM model outperformed previous models in terms of domain independence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attention-based hierarchical LSTM model outperformed previous models in classifying discourse act sequences in social media conversations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical LSTM with word relevance attention mechanism achieved F1-scores of 71% and 66% for predicting discourse roles in Reddit and Facebook discussions, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical LSTM model achieved F1-scores of 71% and 66% for predicting discourse roles of comments in Reddit and Facebook discussions, respectively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model allows for the inquiry into the relevance ordering of text segments according to their roles in discourse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention mechanism allows for the analysis of relevance ordering of text segments according to their roles in discourse.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A human annotator experiment was conducted to unveil important observations about modeling and data annotation.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize a human annotator experiment or corresponding observations, so this specific contribution is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates how heterogeneous non-textual features like location and time characterize online discussions on Facebook.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates domain independence by not relying on platform-dependent structural features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CES outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by over 16% in f-measure on the EDUB-Seg dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CES outperforms the state-of-the-art methodologies by over 16% in f-measure on the EDUB-Seg dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our framework soundly outperforms the state-of-the-art methods for both AU detection and AU intensity estimation on the challenging BP4D, DISFA, FERA 2015, and BP4D+ benchmarks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our framework works well under severe occlusions and large poses.
alignments.rationale: The claim is present in both, but the publication reports it with slightly lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can be easily extended for AU intensity estimation without changing the network architecture.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific claim about easy extension without architecture changes; instead it introduces a different contribution not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative adversarial quantum circuits are a fresh approach to machine learning which may enjoy the practically useful quantum advantage of near-term quantum devices.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the three measures is demonstrated through experiments performed on three online signature datasets and one keystroke dynamics dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology defines the required sample sizes for experiments comparing two optimization methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the definition of desired levels of accuracy for estimates of mean performance differences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method ensures that the accuracy of estimated performance differences is controlled at a predefined level. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two examples demonstrate the application of the proposed method and its ability to achieve desired statistical properties. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: Result preserved with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes inherent memory decay in hidden Markov models to enable efficient inference over long sequences of observations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes inherent memory decay in hidden Markov models to enable efficient inference over long sequences of observations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The length of subsequences $B$ is determined by the formula $B=\frac{\text{log}(\text{error})}{\text{gap of the top two Lyapunov exponents}}$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The length of subsequences B is approximately log(ϵ) / (λ 2 − λ 1) given the controlled error ϵ.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same formula but frames it as an approximation with controlled error (ϵ), which clarifies precision without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm proposed for estimating the gap of the top two Lyapunov exponents is both efficient and accurate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient and accurate algorithm is proposed to numerically estimate the gap after the soft-max parametrization.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the efficiency/accuracy claim but specifies the numerical estimation after a soft-max parametrization, adding methodological detail (clarification).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The continuity of the Lyapunov spectrum allows the estimated subsequence length $B$ to be reused for nearby parameters during inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The continuity of the Lyapunov spectrum ensures that the estimated B could be reused for nearby parameters during inference.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about continuity and reuse of B with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for unsupervised 3D object discovery, reconstruction, and localization does not rely on segmentation, scene knowledge, or user input, making it easily scalable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for unsupervised 3D object discovery, reconstruction, and localization does not rely on segmentation, scene knowledge, or user input, making it easily scalable.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes appearance and geometry of salient regions to find recurrent patterns in a single RGB-D image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method successfully extracts keypoints and matches them in pairs based on their descriptors to minimize false matches.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes a more specific implementation (keypoint extraction and descriptor matching) than the preprint's broader description of using appearance and geometry, and presents it with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatically generated object models are both compact and descriptive.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automatically generated object models are both compact and descriptive.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried over identically from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method has been demonstrated in an object picking scenario with a robotic arm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates effectiveness in an object picking scenario with a robotic arm.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same demonstration but with slightly reduced stated certainty (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The geometry of rational maps from a projective space of an arbitrary dimension to the product of projective spaces of lower dimensions is discussed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The geometry of rational maps from a projective space of an arbitrary dimension to the product of projective spaces of lower dimensions is discussed.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An algebro-geometric variant of the projective reconstruction theorem by Hartley and Schaffalitzky is provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An algebro-geometric variant of the projective reconstruction theorem by Hartley and Schaffalitzky is provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this contribution over identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Distill-and-Compare approach allows for auditing black-box risk scoring models by training transparent student models to mimic their risk scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Distill-and-Compare approach allows for auditing black-box risk scoring models without accessing their APIs or pre-defining features.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds operational detail (no API access or pre-defined features needed) to the original auditing claim, clarifying applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences between the distilled student model and an un-distilled model trained on ground-truth outcomes provide insights into the black-box model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mimic model trained with distillation provides insights into the black-box model by comparing it to an un-distilled transparent model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result in equivalent terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A statistical test was proposed to determine if a data set is missing key features used to train the black-box model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A statistical test was proposed to identify if a data set is missing key features used to train the black-box model.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same methodological contribution as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The statistical test indicates that the ProPublica data is likely missing key feature(s) used in the COMPAS model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The statistical test indicates that the ProPublica data is likely missing key feature(s) used in the COMPAS model.
alignments.rationale: The specific empirical finding is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can generalize well despite its large capacity and complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of new classification and regression algorithms based on deep learning has led to a revolution in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's focused claim about generalization into a broader, more emphatic statement about the impact of deep learning; this changes emphasis and broadens scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses approaches to provide non-vacuous generalization guarantees for deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of a theoretical foundation to guarantee the success of deep learning algorithms is an active research topic in applied mathematics.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey that building theoretical foundations for deep learning generalization is an active and central research direction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical observations in this paper lead to the proposal of new open problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication (a book) does not include an explicit claim that the work proposes new open problems, so this specific preprint claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses the limitations of the results presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention discussion of limitations, so this preprint limitation statement is not carried over.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ICDAR Robust Reading Competition has become a de-facto evaluation standard for robust reading systems and algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICDAR Robust Reading Competition has become a de-facto evaluation standard for robust reading systems and algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Robust Reading Competition Annotation and Evaluation Platform is a modular framework that is fully accessible through on-line interfaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Robust Reading Competition Annotation and Evaluation Platform is a modular framework that is fully accessible through on-line interfaces.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication with no alteration in meaning or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform comprises a collection of tools and services for managing all processes involved with defining and evaluating a research task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform comprises a collection of tools and services for managing all processes involved with defining and evaluating a research task.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same description of the platform's composition and purpose.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many of the tools provided by the framework are generic by design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many of the tools provided by the framework are generic by design.
alignments.rationale: The observation about tool generality appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All aspects of the RRC Annotation and Evaluation Framework are available for research use.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate this claim about universal availability for research use, so the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Deep Multimodal Attention Networks (DMAN) effectively captures the correlations between multimodal contents in social images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Deep Multimodal Attention Networks (DMAN) effectively captures the correlations between multimodal contents in social images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DMAN employs a novel Siamese-Triplet neural network to model the links among images for embedding learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DMAN employs a novel Siamese-Triplet neural network to model the links among images for embedding learning.
alignments.rationale: The description of the model architecture is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learnt embedding from DMAN captures both multimodal contents and nonlinear network information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learnt embedding from DMAN captures both multimodal contents and nonlinear network information.
alignments.rationale: The reported properties of the learned embedding are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DMAN achieves significant improvement in multi-label classification and cross-modal search compared to state-of-the-art image embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DMAN achieves significant improvement in multi-label classification and cross-modal search compared to state-of-the-art image embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is carried over identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Differential Evolution algorithm demonstrates fast convergence speed and improved runtime complexity for protein folding optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Differential Evolution algorithm demonstrates fast convergence speed and improved efficiency for protein folding optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different wording ("efficiency" vs "runtime complexity"), which softens/specifies the original technical phrasing but keeps the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The component reinitialization method mitigates the problem of being trapped in local optima.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate this mechanism-level claim about component reinitialization mitigating local optima; it is not found in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is superior to other state-of-the-art algorithms, achieving a hit ratio of 100% for sequences up to 18 monomers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm obtained a hit ratio of 100% for sequences up to 18 monomers within a budget of 1011 solution evaluations.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 100% hit-ratio claim but adds the evaluation-budget detail, making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New best-known solutions were obtained for most of the amino acid sequences analyzed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: New best-known solutions were obtained for most of the amino acid sequences analyzed.
alignments.rationale: This result is reported the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existence of symmetric best-known solutions is demonstrated in the paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of symmetric best-known solutions is demonstrated in the paper.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this conclusion with higher certainty (publication labels it as a high-certainty result), so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed privacy-preserving sensing framework effectively manages access to time-series data while protecting individuals' privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a privacy-preserving sensing framework for managing access to time-series data that provides utility while protecting individuals' privacy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution to emphasize utility in addition to privacy, clarifying the intended tradeoff without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Replacement AutoEncoder algorithm transforms sensitive features into non-sensitive features, thereby protecting users' privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Replacement AutoEncoder algorithm transforms discriminative features of data corresponding to sensitive inferences into features observed in non-sensitive inferences to protect users' privacy.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more precise description of what features are transformed (discriminative features tied to sensitive inferences), clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The replacement method eliminates the possibility of recognizing and detecting sensitive inferences, addressing weaknesses in traditional approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our replacement method eliminates the possibility of recognizing and detecting sensitive inferences, addressing the main weakness of other approaches such as filtering or randomization.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion but clarifies the comparison to other approaches, making the claim more explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm retains recognition accuracy comparable to state-of-the-art techniques while preserving the privacy of sensitive information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm retains the recognition accuracy of state-of-the-art techniques while simultaneously preserving the privacy of sensitive information.
alignments.rationale: The performance-and-privacy tradeoff is presented equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of GANs for detecting the occurrence of replacement is effective only when the adversarial network is trained on the users' original data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that detecting the occurrence of replacement can only be done if the adversarial network is trained on the users' original data.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same conditional observation about GAN-based detection, with consistent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble of recurrent neural networks (RNNs) can distinguish between normal sinus rhythms, atrial fibrillation, other types of arrhythmia, and noisy signals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble of recurrent neural networks (RNNs) can distinguish between normal sinus rhythms, atrial fibrillation, other types of arrhythmia, and noisy signals.
alignments.rationale: The classification capability is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel task formulation that utilizes the natural segmentation of ECG signals into heartbeats significantly reduces the number of time steps per sequence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel task formulation that utilizes the natural segmentation of ECG signals into heartbeats significantly reduces the number of time steps per sequence.
alignments.rationale: The contribution regarding task formulation is reproduced without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNNs are extended with an attention mechanism that enhances interpretability and decision-making based on specific heartbeats.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of an attention mechanism in the RNNs enhances interpretability while achieving state-of-the-art classification performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes interpretability but also ties the attention mechanism to achieving state-of-the-art performance, broadening the original focus on interpretability and decision-making.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves state-of-the-art classification performance with an average F1 score of 0.79 on an unseen test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved an average F1 score of 0.79 on an unseen test set consisting of 3,658 recordings.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same performance metric but adds the exact size of the unseen test set, clarifying the evaluation details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method increases the spatial resolution of hyperspectral images by fusing them with higher spatial resolution images from different imaging modalities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method increases the spatial resolution of hyperspectral images by fusing them with higher spatial resolution images from a different imaging modality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with only minor wording differences; meaning and scope are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can realign the given images if needed, accommodating for possible mis-registrations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can realign the given images if needed by solving a non-convex blind super-resolution problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the mechanism (solving a non-convex blind SR problem), clarifying how realignment is achieved while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the non-convexity is negligible in practice and reliable solutions can be computed using various optimization algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the non-convexity is negligible in practice and reliable solutions can be computed using various optimization algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental finding verbatim; no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results show the potential of the proposed method and suggest that it is robust with respect to regularization parameters, mis-registration, and the shape of the kernel. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results show that the proposed method is robust with respect to regularization parameters, mis-registration, and the shape of the kernel. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens the language from "show the potential" and "suggest" to a firmer assertion of robustness, increasing the strength of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multilayer nonlinear semi-nonnegative matrix factorization method models user-item interactions more accurately than classical matrix factorization techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multilayer nonlinear semi-nonnegative matrix factorization method models user-item interactions more accurately than classical matrix factorization techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multilayer nonlinear Semi-NMF approach learns latent factors for representations of users and items using explicit ratings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multilayer nonlinear Semi-NMF approach learns latent factors for representations of users and items from explicit ratings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Only minor wording change ('using' vs 'from'), claim content and certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed approach achieves better generalization ability in prediction compared to deep-learning algorithms like Restricted Boltzmann Machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed approach achieves better generalization ability in prediction compared to deep-learning algorithms like Restricted Boltzmann Machine and state-of-the-art Deep Matrix factorization techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the comparison to include additional (state-of-the-art deep MF) methods, strengthening and broadening the original generalization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation ability of our proposed method is comparable to that of state-of-the-art Deep Matrix factorization techniques in the clustering task. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation ability of our proposed approach is comparable to that of deep matrix factorization in the clustering task. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparison in slightly different wording; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Single Shot Action Detector (SSAD) network effectively detects action instances in untrimmed video without the need for proposal generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Single Shot Action Detector (SSAD) network effectively detects action instances in untrimmed video without the need for proposal generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SSAD significantly outperforms other state-of-the-art systems in temporal action detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SSAD significantly outperforms other state-of-the-art systems in temporal action detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same performance claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean Average Precision (mAP) increased from 19.0% to 24.6% on THUMOS 2014 and from 7.4% to 11.0% on MEXaction2 when using SSAD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean Average Precision (mAP) increased from 19.0% to 24.6% on THUMOS 2014 and from 7.4% to 11.0% on MEXaction2 when using SSAD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact numerical results are preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing models for temporal action detection are inadequate for direct adoption, necessitating the empirical search for the best SSAD network architecture. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boundaries of action instance proposals are fixed during the classification step in the traditional 'detection by classification' framework. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both are limitations about prior approaches, but the publication states a specific technical limitation (fixed proposal boundaries) rather than the preprint's broader claim about inadequacy and need for empirical search.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hybrid variant of Differential Evolution (DE) is introduced that combines aspects of Stochastic Quasi-Gradient (SQG) methods to improve optimization efficiency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hybrid variant of Differential Evolution (DE) is introduced to improve optimization efficiency for simulation-based engineering design problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies an application domain (simulation-based engineering design), narrowing the originally more general contribution while preserving the core method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new derivative-free algorithm performs excellently on 'difficult' test functions under tight function evaluation budget constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new derivative-free algorithm outperforms other state-of-the-art DE variants on 25 benchmark functions under tight function evaluation budget constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies and strengthens the performance claim (specific benchmarks and explicit outperforming), clarifying and amplifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The operations used in the proposed mutation scheme are computationally inexpensive and can be easily implemented in existing differential evolution variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mutation scheme is computationally inexpensive and can be easily implemented in existing optimization algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same practical claim about low computational cost and ease of implementation with minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The described concepts can serve as a useful addition to the algorithmic operators in heuristics and evolutionary optimization frameworks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concepts described in the paper can enhance algorithmic operators in heuristics and evolutionary optimization frameworks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same concluding idea about the utility of the concepts with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for 2D vision-based recognition and pose estimation of surgical instruments generalizes to different surgical applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for 2D vision-based recognition and pose estimation of surgical instruments generalizes to different surgical applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel scene model allows for simultaneous recognition of multiple instruments and their parts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel scene model allows for simultaneous recognition of multiple instruments and their parts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contribution without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates good performance for both instrument detection and tracking without the need for scale-dependent sliding window evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach demonstrates good performance for both instrument detection and tracking without the need for scale-dependent sliding window evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method surpasses state-of-the-art results on in-vivo retinal microsurgery image data and ex-vivo laparoscopic sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method surpasses state-of-the-art results on in-vivo retinal microsurgery image data and ex-vivo laparoscopic sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same empirical performance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified Online Goal Babbling and Direction Sampling schemes successfully learn inverse statics mappings for various robotic arms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient approach to learn inverse statics models for gravity compensation can be achieved by exploring only a small part of the configuration space and exploiting symmetry properties.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's specific schemes as an efficient approach that works by exploring a small configuration subset and using symmetry; this clarifies the mechanism while preserving the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of samples required for learning inverse statics models can be drastically reduced by exploiting symmetries in the mappings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Symmetric configurations that require the same absolute motor torques can be discovered and used to generate multiple training samples from one sampled configuration-torque pair.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes explicit the mechanism (discovering symmetric configurations to generate multiple samples) that underlies the preprint's claim of sample reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sample reduction for the 2R and 3R arms can be at least a factor of 8 and 16 respectively, depending on the number of discovered symmetries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of samples required for learning inverse statics mappings for 2R and 3R manipulators can be reduced by factors of approximately 8 and 16, respectively, depending on the number of discovered symmetries.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative sample-reduction factors and conditions, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Exploiting symmetries for learning inverse statics models is a generally applicable strategy for both online and offline learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: This general-applicability statement appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it extends the scope to both online and offline algorithms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing NLDR methods, such as Isomap, fail when the input data is sampled from multiple manifolds or irregularly sampled from a single manifold.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing NLDR methods, such as Isomap, fail when the input data is sampled from multiple manifolds or irregularly sampled from a single manifold.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is able to learn effectively in the presence of multiple, and potentially intersecting, manifolds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is able to learn effectively in the presence of multiple, and potentially intersecting, manifolds.
alignments.rationale: The result about learning with multiple/intersecting manifolds is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the input data to arrive as a massive stream.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the input data to arrive as a massive stream.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's contribution about handling massive streaming input.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The class confusion patterns in CNNs follow a hierarchical structure over the classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The class confusion patterns in CNNs follow a hierarchical structure over the classes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this primary empirical finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchy of similar classes dictates the confusion patterns between the classes and the learning behavior of CNNs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchy of similar classes dictates both the confusion patterns and the learning behavior of CNNs.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same claim about hierarchy influencing confusion and learning behavior with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early layers in CNNs develop feature detectors that can separate high-level groups of classes well after a few training epochs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early layers in CNNs develop feature detectors that can separate high-level groups of classes well after a few training epochs.
alignments.rationale: The statement about early-layer feature development is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Latter layers in CNNs require substantially more epochs to develop specialized feature detectors that can separate individual classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchy of similar classes dictates both the confusion patterns and the learning behavior of CNNs.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the epoch-specific detail for latter layers explicitly but subsumes it under the broader claim that the class hierarchy governs learning behavior; mapping is thus more general/clarifying.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hierarchy-aware CNNs can significantly improve accuracy by accelerating model convergence and alleviating overfitting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hierarchy-aware CNNs can significantly improve accuracy by accelerating model convergence and alleviating overfitting.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion about hierarchy-aware models improving accuracy and convergence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods help in identifying various quality issues in the training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an application-oriented claim (identifying data quality issues) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A readout network can classify objects from their perceptual manifold representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A readout network can classify objects from their perceptual manifold representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about readout classification of manifold representations unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study develops a statistical mechanical theory for the linear classification of manifolds with arbitrary geometry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed statistical mechanical theory reveals a relation to the mathematics of conic decomposition.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a specific mathematical relation (conic decomposition) that clarifies the theory's connections relative to the preprint's broader description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel geometrical measures of manifold radius and manifold dimension can explain the classification capacity for manifolds of various geometries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Special anchor points on the manifolds can define novel geometrical measures of radius and dimension.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that special anchor points define the geometrical measures, which clarifies the preprint's description while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a scaling relation between label sparsity and manifold radius that affects the classification capacity of manifolds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a universal scaling relation between label sparsity and the manifold radius.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim by calling the relation "universal;" semantically this clarifies and the reported confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical predictions of the study are corroborated by numerical simulations using algorithms for maximum margin solutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical predictions are corroborated by numerical simulations using algorithms for maximum margin solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication corroborates the theory with the same numerical simulation evidence as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Examining locomotion has improved our basic understanding of motor control and aided in treating motor impairment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Examining locomotion has improved our basic understanding of motor control and aided in treating motor impairment.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this high-level conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mice and rats are premier models of human disease and increasingly the model systems of choice for basic neuroscience.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mice and rats are premier models of human disease and increasingly the model systems of choice for basic neuroscience.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SLIC superpixels method for segmentation was superior because the segmentation was more reliable and based on both color and spatial information.
